ops.title: cookiesync v0.10.3 bounded every cross-boundary wait ‚Äî union peer-read timeout, ssh subtree kill, auth_status locked-keybag fast path ‚Äî root-caused from a hung getaway session

ops.body:
A getaway balance-refresh session hung on a `cookiesync cookies <14 hosts>` pull; the cascade got misdiagnosed as "airlines use IndexedDB auth." Real root cause: an unbounded cross-host wait, one of three the same incident surfaced. All fixed in **cookiesync v0.10.3** (2026-07-12, tag on main, Release workflow green, formula published to tap; both Macs upgraded).

- **Union peer-read hang (the trigger).** `cookiesync cookies` with no `--browser` unions local browsers AND remote peers over ssh. The peer leg (`Daemon.remoteGetCookies`, `internal/daemon/handlers.go`) carried its own inline `context.WithTimeout(ctx, DispatchTimeout-30s)` = **~9.5 min** (copied from the consent window). A stalled peer (slow ssh past TCP connect, or unapproved routed consent) blocked the whole CLI silently ‚Äî "waiting isn't failing" so the union's skip-with-warning never fired. FIX: new `var unionReadTimeout = 15*time.Second` bounds just that leg; a timed-out peer is skipped with a stderr warning naming the host + likely cause. Consent flows keep the full window BY CONSTRUCTION ‚Äî the peer's release flight runs detached under `context.WithoutCancel`, so a pending Touch ID sheet on the peer survives the killed ssh and the NEXT union pull rides the grant. Live-verified: a real union pull with the peer registered returns in ~12s, not 9.5 min. NOTE the seam is `remoteGetCookies`, NOT `SSHBackend.Extract` (that's converge-only).

- **ssh subtree not killed on deadline (latent, real, fixed).** `execSSHRunner.Run` used `exec.CommandContext` default Cancel (SIGKILL to the direct child only), no `WaitDelay`, no process group. Repro CONFIRMED both holes: (a) `Wait` blocks forever past the deadline when a grandchild holds the stdout pipe (copier never sees EOF); (b) the grandchild survives the direct-child kill. FIX: `SysProcAttr{Setpgid:true}` + `Cancel` does negative-pid SIGKILL (ESRCH‚Üíos.ErrProcessDone) + `WaitDelay=5s`. Real-subprocess regression test (fake ssh in t.TempDir spawns a stdout-sharing grandchild) wedges on revert, passes ~0.3s with the fix. The observed 3-HOUR `rpc apply` survivor was the PEER-SIDE process on yasyf-home (its sshd doesn't reap on client death), not a local leak ‚Äî the local holes only fire with ControlMaster/ProxyCommand/ProxyJump (this fleet's ssh config has none). Same latent pattern in synckit's `cmdTransport.start` (bare exec.Command) ‚Äî flagged, not fixed.

- **auth_status hangs on a locked keybag (regression of v0.8.3 OK-with-note).** The doctor's 2s deadline is a CLIENT socket deadline that never crosses the RPC boundary; the dispatcher runs the handler under `DispatchTimeout` = 10 min. On a locked/screen-shared Mac the session probe (`ioreg` then `netstat -anv -p tcp`; the netstat screen-share probe added in `4cc74e8` is the likely slow await) outran 2s ‚Üí "key cache: i/o timeout" FAIL. FIX: `authStatusTimeout = 1500ms` bounds the probe+cache read via `context.WithTimeoutCause`; a probe that outruns the bound reports `keybag_locked:true` and SKIPS the cache read ‚Üí the OK-with-note reply. Uses `context.Cause` + a private sentinel so the bound is told apart from a parent cancel (a departed client is never mislabeled locked). Live-verified on LOCKED yasyf-home: doctor went 1-FAIL ‚Üí 7/7 OK, key-cache line renders OK-with-note in a 0.63s round trip.

Codex review of the whole diff surfaced two low findings, both applied: enriched the deadline-restore error with the underlying cause (`%w (underlying: %w)`), and `ESRCH‚Üíos.ErrProcessDone` in Cancel. A stewardship fix made `internal/tui/TestRemoveConfirmThenRemove` hermetic (it was flocking the real `~/.config/synckit` held by the resident daemon ‚Äî use the `seedMesh` temp-XDG helper).

**Both backlog items RESOLVED in cookiesync v0.10.4** (2026-07-13, tag on main, Release workflow green, cask published; deploy to both Macs pending next `brew upgrade` ‚Äî binaries still 0.10.3). cc-notes `3c889d6` + `864380d` closed against commit `064494d7`.

- **Heal mutex (`3c889d6`).** `healingWrapper.heal` ran the `cache-newkey` Touch ID subprocess while holding `w.mu`, and every reader (`current`/`degraded`/`Wrap`/`Unwrap`) took the same lock ‚Äî a `context` timeout CANNOT interrupt `sync.Mutex.Lock`, so an in-flight heal blocked `auth_status` past its 1.5s bound. **KEY DESIGN LESSON: moving the subprocess OUT of the lock is the WRONG fix.** A 4-round detour (singleflight ‚Üí channel gate ‚Üí healFlight) each introduced a fresh dedup/shutdown/ctx-share race ‚Äî codex caught real bugs every round (process working, not thrashing). The RIGHT fix keeps the original lock-across-subprocess `heal`/`Close` and makes READERS lock-free via `se atomic.Pointer[SecureEnclaveWrapper]` (nil = degraded; written under `w.mu`, loaded lock-free). `Close` must drop-first-then-nil-on-success ‚Äî a failed `cache-dropkey` keeps the key retryable, never a silent success + leak (round-5 codex HIGH; regression test added). Final codex: correct.
- **Probe split (`864380d`).** Premise ("netstat is the slow syscall") REFUTED by measurement (locked yasyf-home: `ioreg` 17ms, `netstat` 4ms ‚Äî netstat the faster). Split done anyway per user ("Split anyway + fix mutex"): new `probeKeybag` (ioreg-only) feeds `handleAuthStatus`; full `ProbeSession` (ioreg+netstat) stays for `whoami`/`request_consent`/`peerIsLive`, which read `ScreenShared` for consent routing. A cache-read timeout now maps to `degraded:true`/`authenticated:false`, NOT a borrowed `keybag_locked:true` ‚Äî that masking was exactly what the split closes.
- **Two NEW pre-existing backlog items** codex surfaced (priority 3, filed): `42a9649` KeyCache.EvictAll can wipe a concurrent different-endpoint Put (non-atomic); `c177483` SecureEnclaveWrapper.Close ignores the `cache-dropkey` exit code. **BOTH RESOLVED in cookiesync v0.11.1** (2026-07-13, commit `1685628`, tasks closed). `42a9649`: the READ side (`Get`) was ALREADY wrapper-aware (`e.wrapper != c.inner()`), so the fix makes EVICT symmetric ‚Äî `KeyCache.heal` calls `evictStale(keep)` dropping only entries whose wrapper `!= keep`. KEY: pass the just-installed wrapper as `keep`, do NOT re-read `c.inner()` inside evictStale ‚Äî a codex round-2 refuter caught that a `c.inner()`-based evictStale reintroduces a Close-ABA (a racing shutdown `Close` reverts `se‚Üínil`, so `c.inner()` flips back to memoryWrapper and stale entries are spared then served). Do NOT "hold `c.mu` across swap+evict" (codex's other suggestion) ‚Äî that reholds `c.mu` across the heal subprocess = the v0.10.4 hang again. `c177483`: `Close` now errors on nonzero `result.Code`. **But the fix is INERT end-to-end** ‚Äî the signed Swift helper `cacheDropkey` (main.swift:468) discards `SecItemDelete`'s status and always `return 0`, so no nonzero drop code is ever emitted; the Go fix is forward-compatible only. Filed `c67aed3` (helper must surface the status; needs a Developer-ID re-sign/notarize/cask re-release, separate pipeline). Lesson: verify the WHOLE contract chain (Go + helper), not just the Go layer. Also filed `b2e6be7` (LOW): the lazy non-snapshot cache has momentary Get/Put ABA windows during shutdown Close ‚Äî benign (correct key value, no off-box leak, worst case one redundant re-prime), consciously scoped out; a generation-token redesign is the option if Close ever becomes non-shutdown.
- **SHIP SAGA ‚Äî version collision with a concurrent session (durable lesson).** A PARALLEL Claude session was committing + tagging releases on the SAME colocated jj working tree during this session. It committed its watch-refactor + synckit-v0.11.0 work INTO the shared working copy (appeared mid-session; my session-start `jj st` had only `.claude/settings.json`), then tagged webstorage=`v0.10.5` and watch=`v0.11.0` while I was preparing my own v0.10.5. I collided TWICE: (1) v0.10.5 was taken (their webstorage); (2) I then chose v0.10.6 ‚Äî but that is LOWER than their already-released v0.11.0, and my Release ran far enough before I cancelled it to publish a v0.10.6 GitHub release AND push a "Brew cask update ‚Ä¶ v0.10.6" commit, DOWNGRADING the Homebrew cask from 0.11.0‚Üí0.10.6. Recovered: cut `v0.11.1` (correct next-after-v0.11.0; my cache commit sits on top of the v0.11.0 commit), whose Release re-bumped the cask to 0.11.1, then `gh release delete v0.10.6 --cleanup-tag`. **LESSON 1: before choosing a release version, `git fetch --tags` and read the FULL existing tag landscape ‚Äî a concurrent session may have claimed multiple versions out of patch sequence (they jumped 0.10.5‚Üí0.11.0).** **LESSON 2: to ship only your files when the shared tree holds someone else's uncommitted work, use a scoped `jj commit PATHS ‚Ä¶` (or here the parallel session had already extracted their work into its own commit, leaving my `@` clean). `ccx vcs ship` commits the WHOLE `@` ‚Äî wrong when the tree is shared.** **LESSON 3: fish `jj commit -m (cat file | string collect)` fails ("bad pattern"); pass the message inline as a fish double-quoted multiline string (no command substitution inside `"‚Ä¶"`).**
- **Ship gotchas.** A concurrent `main` advance (guides-render commit) failed `jj git push` "stale info" ‚Üí `jj git fetch` + `jj rebase -s` onto the new tip; the rebase RESET the working copy and dropped an unrelated uncommitted `.claude/settings.json` edit ‚Üí recovered via `jj restore --from <old-@> .claude/settings.json` (never let a rebase silently discard a working-copy change that isn't part of your commit). On cc-skills, `jj git push` also carried a stray bookmark (`repo-bootstrap-trust`) ‚Äî verify `origin/main` fast-forwarded to the intended commit after any push. Pushed `main` + watched ci.yml BEFORE tagging so a build issue wouldn't force a re-cut release.

**RESIDUE:** yasyf-home's "re-primes Secure-Enclave wrapped on first prime after unlock" path is unexercised (Mac was locked) ‚Äî verify next time it's awake.

Related: [[cookiesync-web-storage]], [[cookiesync-consent-routing-unified]], [[ab-universal-router]], [[cookiesync-daemon-locked-screen]].
